title: Fixture: Gold-tier low-criticality UC for tests
exclusions: Does not validate certificate expiry or TLS cipher suites — informational uptime only.
evidence: Saved search fixture_gold_low_uptime.json archived to platform_ops index monthly.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Fixture maps operational monitoring to SOC 2 availability narrative for test harnesses.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates that forwarder heartbeat gaps are detected within one hour.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Monthly CSV of hosts missing heartbeat events from fixture_gold_low_uptime.json.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A forwarder stops sending internal metrics for 65 minutes; the absence search returns the host.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Planned maintenance suppresses the host in maintenance_windows lookup; search excludes it and returns zero rows.

dataSources: index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd host=* component=Metrics group=per_host_thruput. Fields: host, kb, _time.
app: Splunk Enterprise (built-in _internal metrics), no additional TA required.

splunkbaseApps.name: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux

spl:
index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=Metrics group=per_host_thruput earliest=-65m
| stats latest(_time) AS last_seen BY host
| eval gap_minutes=round((now()-last_seen)/60,0)
| where gap_minutes > 60
| lookup maintenance_windows host OUTPUT in_window
| where isnull(in_window) OR in_window!="yes"
description: Identifies Splunk forwarders that have not emitted internal throughput metrics for more than sixty minutes, indicating a silent or stopped agent.
value: Quiet forwarders create blind spots in security and operations dashboards. Detecting gaps early prevents data-loss incidents from going unnoticed until audit time.
grandmaExplanation: We check that each data collector is still checking in every hour. When one goes quiet, we flag it so someone can restart it before we lose visibility.
implementation: Schedule the search hourly over the last 65 minutes, email platform team when results appear, maintain maintenance_windows lookup for planned outages.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Search head can read index=_internal; role includes internal indexes.
• maintenance_windows.csv lookup deployed with host and in_window columns.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
No extra inputs — rely on splunkd Metrics on each forwarder. Confirm `_internal` retention covers at least 7 days.

Step 2 — Create the search
Use stats latest(_time) to find stale hosts; filter maintenance via lookup. Understanding this SPL: 65m window avoids false positives from brief restarts.

Step 3 — Validate
Stop splunkd on a lab forwarder for 70 minutes; confirm host appears. Restart and confirm disappearance on next run.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Weekly report to platform team; no paging unless gap exceeds 4 hours (separate high-severity UC).

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
All hosts missing: search head clock skew or wrong index filter. Lookup errors: verify transforms.conf stanza and CSV permissions.
visualization: Table of silent hosts with gap_minutes and last_seen; single-value count; bar chart by deployment serverclass if available.
knownFalsePositives: Forwarders in airplane mode during shipping or decommissioned hosts still in server_inventory — filter via active flag lookup.
hardware: Universal Forwarder fleet in fixture lab.
